properties.remark: NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG CO RD 103...CO RD 3 AND 103, AND ON CO RD 120.

LOTS OF DIME SIZED HAIL WITH A FEW GOLF BALLS. THE LARGER HAIL STONES HAD SPIKES. ALSO RECEIVED 0.80 INCHES OF RAIN.

MANY TREES DOWN IN TOWN, ALONG WITH POWER LINES. LARGE TREES WITH WINDS IN EXCESS OF 60 MPH. REPORTED BY STATE FIRE MARSHALL IN TOWN.
LARGE TREES DOWN, AND METAL ROOFS TORN OFF. ITEMS FOR THEIR FOURTH OF JULY FESTIVITIES BLOWN ALL OVER THE MAIN STREET IN TOWN.
LARGE TREES BROKE OFF IN THE MIDDLE WITH SOME FLOODING REPORTED
DOWNTOWN GILBERT. ALSO MEASURED 54 MPH WINDS. RAINFALL OF 1.88 INCHES BETWEEN 8 PM AND 10 PM.

MOSTLY NICKEL SIZE HAIL...A FEW LARGE TREES DOWN.
A QUARTER MILE LONG SWATH OF TREES UP TO 2.5 FT IN DIAMETER DOWN.
DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE WITH TREES DOWN IN AN AREA AROUND SAND LAKE, ALONG COUNTY ROADS 106, 65, 461 AND 668.
WATER SPOUT WAS SPOTTED 2 MILES OFF OF PARK POINT AND WAS MOVING SSW TOWARDS PARK POINT. THE WATER SPOUT DISSIPATED BY APPROX. 1110 AM BEFORE REACHING LAND.
WATER SPOUT WAS SPOTTED 2 MILES OFF OF PARK POINT FROM SKY HARBOR AIRPORT AND WAS MOVING SSW TOWARDS PARK POINT. THE WATERSPOUT MADE LANDFALL AT PARK POINT NEAR THE AIRPORT.

A WATERSPOUT MADE LANDFALL ON PARK POINT AND MOVED OVER THE SKY HARBOR AIRPORT. THERE WAS NO DAMAGE...HOWEVER 2 FLOATS...WEIGHING ABOUT 500 POUNDS EACH...WERE PICKED UP AND ROTATED. THE TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 30 TO 40 FEET WIDE AND HAD A PATH LENGHT OF .18 MILE. RATING IS AN EF0
THE WATERSPOUT CONTINUED AFTER PASSING OVER PARK POINT. IT WAS ON THE WATER FOR .54 MILES BEFORE MAKING LANDFALL BRIEFLY AT BARKERS ISLAND.
THIS WAS A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AS THE WATERSPOUT MADE LANDFALL AGAIN AT BARKERS ISLAND. THERE WAS NO DAMAGE.
OCCURRED AT NELSON LAKE.
POWER WAS OUT TO 2500 CUSTOMERS FROM COOK TO ELY AS THUNDERSTORMS WITH HIGH WINDS TOPPLED TREES AND BRANCHES ONTO POWER LINES.
